assessment.screening_summary: Patient demonstrates limited verbal fluency output with minimal elaboration, fragmented responses, and reliance on examiner prompting. No evidence of coherent narrative or scene description. Task completion is incomplete and lacks depth. Suggests possible early cognitive concern warranting further clinical review.
evidence_for_risk: Sparse, repetitive animal naming without elaboration
Absence of contextual or narrative content
evidence_against_risk: Patient correctly names several common animals
No signs of agitation, confusion, or emotional dysregulation
Task is completed with minimal prompting
alternative_explanations: Task may be misinterpreted or poorly explained to patient
Patient may be fatigued, distracted, or unfamiliar with verbal fluency task
Language or cultural background may affect response style
risk_control_suggestions: Repeat verbal fluency task with clearer instructions or visual cues
Assess for attention, motivation, and task comprehension
Consider brief cognitive screening battery if clinical context permits
assessment.future_risk_trend_summary: Longitudinal prediction not possible from single clip; follow-up assessment advised
context_notes: Patient speech is minimal and task-dependent
No visual cues available to assess engagement or affect
assessment.disclaimer: Research demo only. This output is not a diagnosis and must not be used as a standalone medical decision.
